Text: I think the United Kingdom should reduce spending on foreign aid because I believe that the foreign aid money should be used to aid people in the United Kingdom as I was brought up to believe charity begins at home. This was always taught to me at school in the 1960s and if we wanted to donate money to other places, i.e. Oxfam, we had to raise the money ourselves. If we look at all the money that has been sent overseas, it has never fixed the lack of water, food, clothes, shelters, etc.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think the UK should reduce spending on foreign aid because I do not feel we are completely stable ourselves as a country. Our roads are in poor repair, our NHS is crumbling, we are in debt, and I believe that we should sort out our own problems first before spending money we do not have on aid for other countries.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: Imagine Russia attacks the UK. We are an island, we are trapped. USA isn't interested in helping and the far right are in power across the EU and are now only inward looking. Where do we go? What do we do? I am an accountant, my partner a teacher. We flee with nothing. What if we come up against your views, what happens to us next? We understood Ukraine as they 'looked like us' but having a different culture, different climate, so looking and living differently does not mean that people who are fleeing are not like you and me.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: The UK should not encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence. While some argue that continued fossil extraction is necessary for economic stability and energy security, this position overlooks the urgent need to address climate change, the volatility of global fossil fuel markets, and the long-term interests of the UK.  Firstly, supporting new oil and gas projects directly contradicts the UK's legally binding net zero commitments.  Secondly, the pursuit of North Sea oil and gas does not guarantee true energy independence or price stability.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I think that the UK should accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution because it would paint us as a nation and world power bad. Imagine a child, barefoot, holding a torn photo of his parents that have been killed, and instead of accepting that child who has nowhere to go in order to survive, we, known as the United Kingdom, send that child away with several other children like that. How is that going to portray us as a country or nation, and also as humans, seeing people, even if they are from another country, suffering without trying to do anything for them? It would be cruel.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I think the UK government should reduce foreign aid as it is now. The government should target countries that actually need short-term help, say war-torn areas that need help rebuilding houses and hospitals, schools, and basic infrastructure. Year after year we give aid to the same countries who don't need it. India, for instance, a wealthy country now, yet we still give them money. We have issues in this country that could benefit homes and shelters for our servicemen when they leave the forces. Our hospitals are crumbling, schools not fit for purpose.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We are currently buying our energy abroad whereas we can drill in the UK and have our own independence, we currently have the best net zero rating of all the EU if not the world. Surely it does not make sense buying our energy abroad when it can be found under our feet. Families are suffering and we pay the heaviest energy prices in the world. It has to stop!
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: As the UK has been the cause or part of the cause of so many conflicts around the world for so very long, we have a moral duty to help those fleeing such conflicts or the consequences of such conflicts. Other European countries, and other countries worldwide, take far more refugees than the UK does. We must start to open many more legitimate avenues of asylum claims so that the obscene "small boats crossings" become unnecessary.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: Our country is suffering fiscal, long housing and NHS waits. Many asylum seekers pass through many safe countries, where they could claim asylum, but choose to continue to England, which should not be allowed. Also, having previously worked in Immigration, I found many were actually economic migrants.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: The simple thing is the UK has a lot of people that are in need of help, some of which die. They can't heat or feed themselves. The next thing you see is £200,000 going overseas. We have some that just can't work as they are disabled and get no help. This is not right. Overseas get the aid, not anyone in this country that needs it. This is not fair.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I believe that we have significantly given foreign aid to various countries for many years. Some countries such as India are not in need of our contributions. The United Kingdom has a massive problem with undocumented immigrants and the standard of living for the indigenous population is falling. The country has significant debts, failing national health. Our roads are all in a terrible state and money needs to be spent at home. We also have a generation that doesn’t want to work—that, complete with the immigration, is a massive benefit problem needing solving.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We should reduce foreign aid in exchange for domestic aid to fix our roads, our hospitals, our schools, and our public transport. We are falling apart as a nation and it is high time we addressed these everyday, important issues before we try to help others. This isn't about a lack of care for foreign soil, but a recognition of the deep need of our domestic one. Politicians are here to represent the people, not the other way around. Let the people vote on this very important topic in a referendum.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: Because they are bringing in nonces and violent people, prioritising them over our own, especially our elderly veterans and also our disabled people who genuinely need the money for this sort of thing. Some of our homeless are either elderly veterans or disabled people also. Our elderly deserve to be able to live a better life instead of struggling on a day-to-day basis as they are on the minimum to survive, some not having any family. Same goes to the veterans.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think the UK should reduce its spending on foreign aid, in aid to help its own country get back on its feet. It’s easy for people to say it’s only this or that and we can afford it. In truth, we can’t afford it without extra borrowing or increasing taxes to the working people in our country. How much do we receive from other countries to help with the homeless or deprived people in despair, in need of help? It’s about time we started to look after our own first for a change. Adverts begging for money for foreign countries have been going on all my life, and I am now 60. Where has all that money
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I see no issue in having them come into this country if this is done in the correct channel. If they are all monitored and are willing to contribute to the society, there should be no issue. If they refuse to do this, they should be returned back to where they have come from. If they come into this country because they need the help, they should accept the help on the terms given and follow the rules by the government. Anyone that is found to not follow the rules (end up in prison) should be returned and not allowed back.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I think that the UK should not introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions because either every item the consumer buys would be higher priced or manufacturers would falsely claim that the goods were less polluting than they are. It would cause high prices and confusion. Customers would be angry and less inclined to do anything which benefits the environment. It leaves me asking how would the products be taxed and where would this go. To what use would the tax be put? It reminds me of the carrier bag tax. Where does that go? Does anyone know?
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: The country is having financial problems and we can no longer afford to send so much money in foreign aid. Also, some of the countries receiving the aid are in a better financial state than we are. Then there are some countries whose government or leading figure are using the money for their own ends, not using it for the people it was meant for.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We should support overseas aid for poorer countries to make our presence higher in the world and because it is morally right to share our wealth with those who need it.  As a secondary effect, improving the lives of poorer people will take the pressure off migration. These improvements include ensuring all children get at least a minimum education to improve their prospects in the local economy.  We need immigrants to support our lifestyle, but we would prefer them to be educated so they can take up useful positions, not just the low-paid service positions. I believe in controlled migration.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text:
I think the UK should introduce a carbon tax because doing this will drastically reduce emissions, we know how damaging these carbon are, it causes climate damage, air pollution to name a few. With the increase in tax on such activities that cause or produce these carbon would make a balance by making polluting goods more expensive. And like we know the more expensive such goods are the lower the demand will be and the lesser such will be produced which will automatically mean cleaner environment.
Right now the prices of carbon product does not reflect how much they pollute. Take fuel
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I believe an emissions tax should be placed on everyday goods and energy because this will encourage people to think more about their everyday individual actions and the larger impacts they can have on the environment. I think it could lead to better decisions in the supermarket, and could in turn lead to healthier lifestyles as the more sustainable products and services will be prized above profit-chasing products and companies.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I think the UK should stop sending money to other countries, as we don't have the money, it could be better spent on this country, what with all the illegal immigrants coming here we just haven't the money to send abroad, charity begins at home, we have enough homeless people here, and not enough money to help our homeless, or elderly and disabled people.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We have oil and gas in the North Sea and know where it is and how to get it, so why are we paying other countries for their oil and gas at exorbitant prices and have to pay to have it shipped to us? Is this not absolute stupidity? Sorry, I am a man of few words and am not used to waffling on at great length wasting time and energy on irrelevancies.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Great Britain has a wealth of natural resources such as vast gas and oil fields off our coast that can easily be harvested at a much lower cost, both financial and ecological. This would lower the cost of fuel to the public and, combined with reducing the government's tax on fuel, would serve to boost the economy.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: The UK should not introduce a carbon tax as this won't reduce emissions, all it will do is penalise the everyday person trying to go about their life. It will prove too hard to monitor and the 1% are wealthy enough to pay the tax with no consequence. The 1% are also polluters as they fly around on private jets, think Bezos and his like when 200 odd guests used 100 or so private jets to take over a city for his wedding. The everyday person will be penalised for buying something that may not be local to them whilst others fly in delicacies from around the world. Also hard to manage.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I believe that there should be a carbon tax on products that create more carbon during production because that would encourage the manufacturers to invest in cleaner production methods. This would benefit both the consumer and the producer and encourage innovation, which would also bring its own benefits to the country as a whole. We need to find solutions to lower emissions in all walks of life, and big companies could fund the research for their own advantage, but it would also trickle down the system and bring benefits to all of us eventually by making new jobs in research and cleaner product.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: Due to the damage we have caused, not only war and exploitation of other countries, we should support the countries that have helped us. Also, to create a more harmonious world we live in, supporting other countries would help. Surely every human deserves the right to have the basic needs met and we should not live in a world where this does not. There are many places in the world that do not have the same infrastructure and assets to educate and support people, and that should be a must in society.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: The money that is being spent on foreign aid could be put to better use by using it to spend on services in the UK that need updating/more staff etc. to enable the UK to provide better services for its citizens. The NHS, police, council services need to be greatly improved and this money would be put to far better use for the UK rather than being spent on useless projects in other countries, some of which do not need the money and end up wasting it.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: There are numerous resources in the North Sea just waiting to be used. In these times, especially with the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, we need to be able to be independent from reliance on other countries for our gas, etc. We have the technology and therefore we need to explore the North Sea resources. This will aid the economy greatly and take away the fear factor of the current situation happening again. We could also benefit the economy by selling to other countries any excess we don't need ourselves. All in all, a win-win situation.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: This country cannot afford to keep helping other countries with handouts. I believe that the people who need it do not receive it; it just continues to fill their government pockets. We cannot look after our own people or support them to the best of our ability as it is. We keep paying more taxes to cover every expense and we do not benefit our own people that need it. We need to uphold our NHS. Something has to give and foreign aid should be the first to stop.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think we should reduce the amount we spend on foreign aid, as we as a country are never given any assistance when we need it. We are not a rich country, yet seem to send all our money to foreign aid and get nothing when we are in crisis. I don't see why other countries' financial situations are our problem, why do we need to help them when their government don't want to - as they say - let them.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think the UK should not accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution as the responsibilities of the UK should be primarily to support British citizens. Public spending is already stretched due to high levels of immigration, people on benefits, publicly funded National Health Service and schools, etc. I think there are other ways the UK can support those who are persecuted in other countries without allowing them free access to the benefits of living in the UK.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: We have no control over how our Foreign Aid is spent in these countries, it is wasted on vanity projects by these countries or worse on International Space programmes, a space programme—how in any world is that benefiting the people that it's supposed to benefit the most, i.e., the people in need without basic access to sanitary facilities or to enable them to be educated to grow their own food or to help them understand how things work to enrich their lives so that they feel more worthy and give them the self-esteem to feel they are valued people in the world and they are contributing.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think we have enough issues in our own country that need to be fixed and prevented first. This would be helped by reducing foreign aid, as the more cash we keep here the better for our own environment and communities. There are many organisations that need support much more than other places, and many people would love more.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I believe it's everyone's responsibility to reduce emissions. Many people don't believe in climate change; they also don't think that, as individuals, they can make a difference. Therefore, people don't make the effort to make the small but significant changes that could help. Competing political parties jump on the bandwagon to gain votes by dismissing climate change and net zero targets. Businesses only think of profits and not the environment. Something needs to be done to ensure a clean and healthy future for the next generations. It is a price we all need to pay for our negligence.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: We now have a major immigration problem in this country stoked by decades of political mismanagement. The money we used to give to foreign aid is now needed in this country. The problems that foreign aid solved have migrated into this country too. We cannot support both. We need to prioritise aid so that Great Britain comes first.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: My argument is the money the United Kingdom sends to foreign aid such as Ukraine and many other places around the world is that the United Kingdom is on its knees with so many cuts to public sectors, yet we can come to the aid of lands we don't live on, fights that are not ours to get involved in. We invest too much in foreign aid. It's time to think of the people of the United Kingdom and put them first.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We pay taxes and should be rewarded for our people first before helping others so it's fair, we deserve our say, give us a say on where the money goes instead of deciding for us. our money, we should have a say in where it goes. I would like to have a say on where my taxes go. Charity begins at home, would rather help the homeless guy down the road than the people I don't know.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: Stopping oil/gas production from the North Sea would be counterproductive to the UK. We're still going to need and use these commodities, and all that would happen is instead of being energy self-efficient we would be reliant on other countries for our energy needs, countries who, if they chose to, could turn off the supply in an instant! Also, our production of gas and oil is a drop in the ocean compared with the USA, Middle East, China, and Russia, so we should definitely continue to search for further oil and gas supplies to secure our energy supplies for the future.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I believe, with evidence supplied from Greenpeace over the decades, that if we continue to invest in poisonous, exploitative North Sea oil rigs, the drought conditions we've seen over this summer of 2025 will grow to a level of no return. Heatwaves will deteriorate our livelihoods, our health, our world. Wildfires will become a daily challenge. People will die at a larger rate. Meanwhile, oil companies do everything in their power to erase and deny climate change, and wash their hands of thousands of deaths which they have caused. Oil investment is no longer a step any of us should consider.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Of course we should explore it all in the North Sea as it is there already for us to use, this will then provide all the energy we need as a country plus more that we can sell to other countries to make money and reduce all our household bills. Will also create a lot of jobs for people in the area.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I think that the UK should not accept more asylum seekers as we are already a country on our knees. Our NHS is in crisis and people are missing out on vital care and operations due to lack of funding as we support not only our own but also those seeking asylum. I also genuinely believe that the previous 'system' of seeking asylum in the first safe country to which you arrive is correct - if you were genuinely seeking asylum where is not relevant, you just need to be safe. I embrace being multicultural, however I fear we are being overrun by people just taking advantage of the benefit state.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: Yes, the UK should accept asylum seekers to preserve their lives as these people, especially Christians, are hunted and killed, there are generations being wiped out, but should conduct proper screening of these people as most asylum seekers come into the country to constitute a nuisance and change the culture and tradition of Britain.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: As much as I want to support the reduction in emissions, I feel that taxing even more items is going to make our lives more of a misery and it's going to add a bigger gap between the poorest and the wealthy. I think there are other ways that the UK can reduce emissions through other movements, such as making public transport cheaper so more people use this instead of individual cars, or reducing packaging so there are fewer emissions coming from factories.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: At this time of conflict and unrest in the world, we should be making a safer and secure future for the next generation. We as a country need to become more self-sufficient and less dependent on other countries. The way forward is to carry out our own explorations in the North Sea to ensure our own energy independence.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: The tax should be on the everyday user, as some people are more efficient than others. The tax should be on the high users and those that do not do the best practices. The taxes should be a commercial tax. I know this will affect the end user, but the end user then has a choice of which brand or company to purchase from.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: A lot more needs to be done for improving the standard of living for people, improve health services, reduce cost of energy, and leave money in people's pocket. Increase expenditure on primary education, public health, and services. Strengthen border and reduce influx of illegal immigrants which is overburdening the available resource.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: Energy in the UK costs around twice the USA's, even more compared to China's. This handicaps industry severely but also citizens, whose costs of living have soared due to Green energy subsidies loaded onto fixed charges. The North Sea resources should be used to help reduce UK prices and dependence on foreign imports. UK CO2 emissions are <1% of the global total, whereas China's are >30% from a 1.4 billion population. Even if you think these may be harmful, the UK share is insignificant. Solar power is not a good option in the UK (clouds!) and wind is unreliable for base load.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: We cannot afford to house, feed, clothe people in our own country without sending money to places like Pakistan for contraception! I agree when a major disaster happens to help, but to tax people for a fund for incidentals is beyond me, especially when there are so many homeless and people in poverty here.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: The UK has an ever-growing population with the majority of working adults living on an income that just about covers their outgoings. People generally don’t mind paying tax that goes towards improving the living conditions for UK citizens. The government does not have a bottomless pit of money and although our taxes are not directly used for foreign aid, it can be assumed and perceived that each working person is indirectly contributing to the foreign aid budget. It is essential that we have a budget to support countries in crisis, but not countries such as China who do not need our support.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: It's about time this country started helping itself more than it has been. When this country has a crisis or emergency, who is there to help us? That's right, no one, we have to manage ourselves. If we helped ourselves more instead of sending money abroad, we would be in a much better position to help others when asked to do so. How many times has any government said that they cannot afford to do something for the people, then magically they produce a couple of million to help another country? Too many to remember. Remember, charity begins at home.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: We should follow the example of most countries in the world and exploit whatever resources are available to us. Our country is economically "challenged" at the moment and whatever streams of revenue there are should be made the most of. We must not cripple the UK by adhering to impossible to achieve targets and political dogma. As a nation we are disappearing into economic insignificance and oil and gas may just be able to save us and our standard of living.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: The government should absolutely invest in manufacturing our own energy. One, to ensure we as a country always have the fuel/power to keep industry and domestic needs plentiful. Two, we should be able to provide cheaper energy, rather than having to line the pockets of other countries which we have to pay for it.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I believe in the UK we should currently be encouraging oil and gas exploration in the North Sea. We can deliver this by producing less pollution, less costs, and keep our country's energy security safe. It is more costly and more polluting to ship it in from other countries. We are also losing jobs and businesses who are going into administration in the north of Scotland due to low oil production and high windfall tax on profit. We should extract from our North Sea fields now and gradually change over to other fuel sources. Put our people, jobs, and communities first.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Expanding oil and gas does not increase our energy independence as the commodity will go on the open market to the highest bidder and is a waste of funds to companies that will also legally avoid paying a fair share of tax. So funds will be wasted to develop liabilities in redundant infrastructure that the taxpayer will be burdened with for decommissioning, clean up, and/or the potential pollution.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Whilst it is tragic that some people are obviously fleeing for their lives from their home countries, it is also obvious that some of these 'migrants' are only travelling to this country to take advantage of our welfare system. Stricter controls need to be introduced to ensure that those who are entering this country fraudulently are removed in a rapid and efficient manner.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I believe that whilst foreign aid is important, the current state of the UK means that the money should be redistributed to help those in need in this country. The services are broken and we need to help public services; this would hopefully kickstart growing the economy so that foreign aid could be brought back in, but currently we must put our country first. I also have doubts about where the foreign aid money is used and whether it is used appropriately, so would like to see more clarity of what the money is used for.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: The cost of importing energy is high and keeps on getting higher. It is wasteful and does not make economic sense to ignore the energy resources we have readily available to us. Using these resources will boost the country's economic position, bring additional income into the country, create jobs, help keep prices down, aiding those who suffer poverty and can least afford high energy prices.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: The UK is facing more and more issues stemming from climate change caused by the continued use of fossil fuels globally. For example, hotter summers have led to poorer harvests for farmers, thus increasing food insecurity. We should be focusing our resources and investment on exploring renewable sources of energy, such as tidal, wind, and solar, that will have less harmful impacts than non-renewable sources. Our nation can be a leader and an example for others to follow in the energy sector.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Being independent on fuel prices should lower the prices for the consumer and their bills over time. Relying on other countries for power fuels increases our pricing and bills. We all need lower bills to survive the cold winters for our country, it is much, much worse now than back in years when we had harsher winters but not much heating. We are worse off now financially due to the rising costs on a permanent basis, taking food from our mouths. Children and the aged are suffering much more on fuel and food rising costs, creating much more health worries, straining the NHS.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I think our country should support itself rather than giving money to other countries first. There are so many people needing help in this country that are struggling so much but not getting the help they need or deserve because our country is too busy sending money and trying to help people from other countries. We should be helping our own first, then maybe help the most deserving people from other countries next, but only when people in our country have been taken care of. We need to look after our own first and foremost. No one from our country should be put last in this situation ever.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: A carbon tax would make polluters pay their fair share, encouraging cleaner choices without heavy regulation. Revenue could help lower-income households through rebates or cheaper green energy. It's not a penalty, but a smart incentive for innovation and fairness. The cost of doing nothing is far higher—for our economy, health, and future.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I would want to highlight that asylum seekers should not be given any chances in the sense that most of them cause nuisance, thereby affecting people who genuinely need that claim. Furthermore, there have been instances where the claims cannot be verified or there is a false alarm where there isn't any trouble. It should be regulated and people who prove worthy should be given.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: Whilst I do agree that emissions should be closely monitored and reduced wherever possible, I don't think that the introduction of a carbon tax is the way to achieve this. A lot of the goods involved in the higher emissions are the cheaper, often lower quality, goods which people on lower incomes rely on. Those with more wealth already have the option to shop around for lower emission products without concern over the price. The introduction of such a tax would most likely put more people below the poverty line, and leave many families unable to keep a roof over their head.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I do not agree with government doing more drilling for gas and oil in the North Sea, yes it would give us independence on foreign gas and oil, but we need to be doing more to help climate change and this is not the way to do it, just making things worse. We are an island and have wave power, it's windy here too so more wind farms and other forms of getting energy not dependent on oil or gas which is bad for the environment. We need to stop and think, look around us and see the devastation caused by severe weather due to climate change. Jamaica is just the latest to be affected but it's everywhere.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: We cannot accept more illegal immigrants/asylum seekers as they are damaging our country. We are letting in rapists, sex offenders, and killers. The majority of the three things listed are done by immigrants. England is now finished. We must stop the boats now. We are in a crisis and the government are doing nothing about it. They get more than our homeless people will ever get. A roof over their head and food paid for. It's just not right.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I think we need to introduce a carbon tax because we can no longer sustain our environment. The planet is dying. Our rivers, seas, and wildlife are dying because we have overexploited carbon underground. We need clean energy, which needs investments to build the new infrastructure, which can come from the carbon tax. Modern technology can build batteries to store energy, and electric cars can now go for longer and longer. Industrial factories can now sustain themselves on very clean energy. We have no more excuses. Small communities in jungles cannot get solar panels. There is hope at last.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I believe that enough is enough with government spending on foreign aid. How can we justify spending loads of money on helping overseas people when we cannot even cover the costs of helping our own people? I think charity very much begins at home and I would urge you to rethink and do the sensible thing and reduce overseas spending.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: The government should be thinking more about their own people rather than other countries and the cost of living crisis, and the energy bills, and the illegal immigrants crisis, crime and racism, radical litigation and knife crime, also be thinking about the safety of young girls and women.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think the UK should reduce spending on foreign aid in some areas as I feel we spend too much on this and some of the places we give money to do not really need it from things I have read. If you can afford a space programme, then in my mind you have enough money to care for your own country. I also feel the way this country is at the moment we should be spending more money on important issues in our own country or helping people, as they say charity begins at home, and there are a lot of good causes in the UK which could get some of the foreign aid that we cut from the current spending cost.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: This won't persuade anyone because we have too many lily-livered saboteurs in our society, but I feel better for venting. The country is on its arse. It's heavily indebted, low on productivity, and run by morons. Text: Foreign aid has long been a central part of the United Kingdom’s international policy, aimed at reducing poverty, promoting development, and supporting humanitarian causes abroad. I strongly believe that the UK should reduce its spending on foreign aid and focus more on addressing domestic needs, ensuring accountability in aid distribution, and promoting self-reliance among recipient nations.

Text: The people coming here illegally are coming from a safe country and have also travelled through many safe countries. They want to come here for the free hotel rooms, food, money allowance, and extras, while our own people have been paying taxes for many years and are now having to support these people out of the taxes they are paying at a time when our government are telling us we have no money and want us to pay more, and the British people are suffering with the high cost of living, the lack of doctors and school places, and housing due to mass immigration on an enormous scale. This has to stop.

Text: Consider the state of conflict or peace in the world, consider the cost to the general public of fuel, consider the UK's dependence on importing fuel from unreliable, potentially unfriendly sources and our lack of ability to produce power other than unreliable green sources. Get oil from the North Sea and fracking and become self-sufficient, safer and wealthier as a country.  The danger to the country and its population, including "the greens," is far closer from losing the ability to produce and transport goods, including food and water, and also keeping warm in winter than global warming events!
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: I am strongly against the UK accepting more asylum seekers because it is posing a great economic and resource strain on the UK economy with regards to housing, health care, education, and even job competition and wages. Also, it poses great risk and national concern for the UK as it has heightened concerns regarding border control and other external factors.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: The UK has allowed too many illegal immigrants in, and allowed them to be free to wander/roam amongst UK citizens. We do not know their history, their political beliefs, plus whether they are murderers, rapists, thieves, etc. Most do not integrate with us. All we see and hear now is the fact they are allowed to stay in hotels or HMOs, given free phones, money, health, dentals, driving lessons, etc. All this when our own people are trying to get hospital appointments, dental appointments, trying to afford to buy a house, etc.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I believe that the UK should still assist asylum seekers coming to the country for the reason of humanitarian purposes. The people came to the country for help and survival, and they can play a vital role in the country if given the chance for them to prove themselves and assist them to show their worth as human beings. How can we deprive them of having a second chance in life if we are not here to assist them and guide them for a better life and future? We can, in the UK, help them within the country rather than spending and sending money to other countries for help if we can help them here in the UK.
Issue: accept more asylum seekers who are fleeing war or persecution.

Text: I strongly support that government should introduce carbon tax. Carbon tax is necessary to help reduce emissions into the air. It is no longer news that climate change is a major global problem. Carbon tax will help UK achieve zero net emissions target by 2030. This will also help curtail or make people aware of the need to protect our planet and ensure peaceful ecosystem.
Issue: introduce a carbon tax on everyday goods and energy that pollute more to reduce emissions.

Text: I think the UK should not reduce spending on foreign aid. Doing so is quite short-sighted and self-defeating. If developed countries assisted the less developed countries, then many of the current issues plaguing the world would be less severe. By maintaining the levels of foreign aid provided, we would see less migration because the migrants would be incentivised to remain in their countries. There would also be less poverty and famine, and eventually these countries won't need as much support as they currently do. Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think that we, the UK, expenditure on foreign aid is a concern that needs scrutiny. With a significant portion of the budget allocated towards foreign aid, it's essential to assess whether this spending is truly beneficial to the nation. To me, in the current economic climate, exercising financial prudence is crucial. Redirecting funds from foreign aid projects to vital sectors like healthcare, education, and small business support could have a more substantial impact on the lives of UK citizens.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think we should encourage oil and gas in the North Sea. Maybe the price would be lower as we don’t need to rely on any other countries. And maybe keep the household fuel down and would help people who are struggling with the household bills and would have more money to spend on food and clothing for their families, especially people with a few children.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Whilst the wealthiest countries of the world should support the less wealthy ones, I no longer think that the UK falls into the bracket of the wealthiest. The UK has myriad shortcomings, financial and otherwise. A recent survey I saw said that between 1 and 3 in 10 children in the UK live below the poverty line. Imo, these children should take priority over children from other countries. Our schools, hospitals, roads, all need huge investments to be made on them to bring them up to acceptable standards. The UK needs to build a lot more houses, but not to buy but to rent at affordable rents.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I think we need this to be independent. We need our own gas securities. I can't understand why there are some people who say don't do this. We clearly need this for ourselves. I can't really make a good argument merely because I am not into the ins and outs of this. I don't too well understand politics etc. but think if we have this, our own bills will be lower and we won't be reliant on someone or a country for our own warmth, fuel, etc.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: In this world of unrest and war we are held to ransom by other nations we have become reliant on for oil and gas. We need to move quickly towards renewables for energy but in the short term need to be self-sufficient to avoid major issues beyond our control. The oil and gas available to us in our waters is limited so cannot be a long-term plan for our country but there will be sufficient for us to be self-sufficient during the period needed to move our renewable energy sources forward in order to move away entirely from our reliance on fossil fuels. Long term the aim must be to be independent.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Let all forget what society makes us think we should say, that caring for others is most important and we should go without to help the less fortunate. At the heart of most of us, we all think NOOO!! We should be looking after ourselves first. Our soldiers are living on the streets, families have to choose between heat or food because of the cost of living. All money should stay in the country to solve our own problems before sending millions to others. We can help others once we have no major life-threatening issues of our own.
Issue: reduce spending on foreign aid.

Text: I agree that more needs to be done to ensure that the UK has more independence in the way it produces or acquires energy, but I do not think that the UK should encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to achieve its energy independence. Not only will it cost a lot of money to increase exploration in the North Sea, but it will also lead to more carbon being pumped into the atmosphere. The UK is committed to reach net-zero by 2050, and to achieve this it will need to increase the use of renewable energy sources like wind, solar, and nuclear energy.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: It's a no-brainer. Why do you need persuading? We will always require oil and gas, perhaps not in the same quantity as today, but required to make wheels/wind turbines to turn (literally). Just because other nations may be greedy and selfish doesn't mean that we have to be.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: Most of the problems we are facing are exacerbated by climate change, so seeking out further fossil fuels is suicide. We get one planet, one environment, and should not waste it. Extreme weather events are becoming more commonplace as the planet warms and we should all do our bit. Even if there are other major polluters out there, if we can lead by example and achieve energy independence without environmental destruction, others will follow. Solar is easy to implement as are other methods, and there are savings to be made in the energy we use. In this century we need modern solutions.
Issue: encourage oil and gas exploration in the North Sea to ensure energy independence

Text: A country should look after its own residents before giving money away to other countries, many of whom are corrupt and the aid doesn't reach the people it's supposed to help. Text: The UK should reduce spending so much on foreign aid, there are enough people, charities and organisations in the UK that should be helped first, too many are so far under the poverty line and not receiving enough help, homelessness is so high and yet being allowed to just 'carry on', support is needed immediately, let's help our own country and our own people before others... please

Text: The country is running out of money. We will be bankrupt before the end of this diabolical government if they carry on with taxing the vulnerable and giving in to the unions. We will have to rely on buying gas and electricity from other countries which we can't afford when we could be getting our own gas etc. from the North Sea and possibly even selling some to other countries and bringing down the debt.
